title: feat(tinybird): expose public stats pipes + docs
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Exposes anonymous, read-only platform stats via Tinybird public token, and documents them in the gen API docs (Scalar).

**Pipes**
- Adds `app_directory_public` — public passthrough of the community app directory (APPS.md); drops internal fields (github_user_id, free-text other)
- `app_top_weekly` — adds public read token; scopes to apps whose owner is a published showcase contributor; corrects description (owner is in showcase, returned app may be any of their registered apps)
- `weekly_health_stats` — adds public read token; corrects availability to `2xx / (2xx + 5xx)` (5xx-only failures, 4xx excluded, cache excluded), matching the model monitor; availability now in percent

**Docs**
- Adds Public Stats section to gen docs (all 5 public pipes, public read token, curl example)
- Reorders Resources sidebar: Safety, Errors, then Public Stats last

Deployed to prod (`pollinations_enter`, deployment #128); all 5 pipes verified returning correct data on the public token. Staging (`pollinations_enter_staging`) still needs the same deploy for parity.

Supersedes #11883 (docs folded in here).

gist.headline: Stats for the swarm
gist.blurb: We've opened up our platform analytics. You can now query live data on top apps, health metrics, and the community directory directly via our public Tinybird token.
gist.summary: Exposed five read-only platform statistics pipes via a public Tinybird token, including a new app_directory_public endpoint, and added a Public Stats section to the API documentation.
gist.impact: Developers can now query live platform health metrics, top weekly apps, and the community app directory directly, with full documentation and curl examples available in the API docs.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot and a tiny pixel bird (representing Tinybird) looking at a glowing retro terminal displaying colorful bar charts and graphs. The theme is data, stats, transparency, and public access.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a tiny blue pixel bird looking at a glowing retro terminal displaying colorful bar charts and graphs. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
